bins.name: Flexible, not prescriptive rule
bins.desc: Eversource argues a strict one-size-fits-all interconnection process would impede rather than facilitate large-load interconnection and could cause more harm than the status quo. It urges sufficient flexibility to address varied load types, hybrid arrangements, and region-specific rules.
quotes: an overly prescriptive and restrictive final rule arising out of this proceeding could result in more harm than the status quo to prospective large load customers, interconnecting transmission owners, and the public generally.
implementing a strict, one-size-fits-all interconnection process will impede rather than facilitate the interconnection of these large loads.
the Commission should also ensure that any final rule provides sufficient flexibility to address the different needs of large-load customers.
Any final rule arising out of this proceeding must be flexible enough to accommodate different load types and their needs.

the final rule should also be flexible enough to encompass different “hybrid” load arrangements, including those co-located with generation seeking standby service, behind-the-meter generation with voluntary curtailment/isolation, and other arrangements that may be sought by customers.

The final rule should also be flexible enough to allow transmission providers to develop and address region-specific rules and concerns (such as termination of co-located generator interconnection rights).

bins.name: Flexibility for hybrid arrangements
bins.desc: The filer wants the rule flexible enough to cover hybrid load arrangements, including co-located generation with standby service and behind-the-meter generation with voluntary curtailment, and to let transmission providers address region-specific concerns such as termination of co-located generator interconnection rights.
quotes: the final rule should also be flexible enough to encompass different “hybrid” load arrangements, including those co-located with generation seeking standby service, behind-the-meter generation with voluntary curtailment/isolation, and other arrangements that may be sought by customers.

The final rule should also be flexible enough to allow transmission providers to develop and address region-specific rules and concerns (such as termination of co-located generator interconnection rights).

bins.name: Co-located load arrangements
bins.desc: Eversource asks the final rule to accommodate loads co-located with generation and to let transmission providers set region-specific rules governing co-located generator interconnection rights.
quotes: the final rule should also be flexible enough to encompass different “hybrid” load arrangements, including those co-located with generation seeking standby service, behind-the-meter generation with voluntary curtailment/isolation, and other arrangements that may be sought by customers.

The final rule should also be flexible enough to allow transmission providers to develop and address region-specific rules and concerns (such as termination of co-located generator interconnection rights).

bins.name: Preserve state retail arrangements
bins.desc: Eversource asks the Commission to preserve existing and prospective retail interconnections at transmission voltages, noting that prospective large loads overwhelmingly prefer the state-jurisdictional retail construct over the federal process.
quotes: the Commission should preserve both existing and prospective retail interconnections at transmission voltages in order to efficiently interconnect large loads to the system.
In Eversource’s experience, prospective large loads overwhelmingly prefer the retail construct.

bins.name: MW threshold above 20 MW
bins.desc: The filer suggests a voltage or MW threshold well above 20 MW could let transmission providers require only the largest loads to use a federal process, but cautions that any fixed threshold invites loads to artificially reduce capacity below the line, causing greater inefficiency.
quotes: the Commission could consider a voltage or MW threshold (likely much greater than 20 MW) such that transmission providers could still require the largest loads to participate in a federal interconnection process.
any specific threshold may result in loads artificially reducing their capacity to just below that threshold to avoid such requirements, which could result in greater inefficiencies overall.

bins.name: Optional cluster study for loads
bins.desc: Eversource opposes mandatory inclusion of loads in the Order No. 2023 cluster study process, arguing its strict and lengthy timelines are infeasible for time-pressured large loads. Inclusion should be optional, and the Commission could consider not including loads at all.
quotes: to the extent any final rule arising out of this proceeding contemplates including qualifying loads in the Order No. 2023-mandated cluster study process, such inclusion should be optional.
the strict and often lengthy study timelines for generator interconnection may not be practically feasible with the tight timelines for the interconnection of large loads
the Commission should consider not including loads in that process at all due to the time pressures described above.

bins.name: No mandatory joint load-generator study
bins.desc: The filer resists studying loads together with generators on a mandatory basis, because generator study timelines cannot meet the tight schedules large loads face. Any inclusion in the joint cluster process should be optional or avoided entirely.
quotes: to the extent any final rule arising out of this proceeding contemplates including qualifying loads in the Order No. 2023-mandated cluster study process, such inclusion should be optional.
the strict and often lengthy study timelines for generator interconnection may not be practically feasible with the tight timelines for the interconnection of large loads
the Commission should consider not including loads in that process at all due to the time pressures described above.

bins.name: Expedited process, voluntary curtailment
bins.desc: Eversource supports an expedited study process for large loads under time pressure or access to state retail processes. It reports its customers show no interest in mandatory dispatchable curtailment, suggesting eligibility be tied to voluntary curtailment instead.
quotes: The Commission could also create an expedited study process for all large loads under significant time pressure, or allow those loads to avail themselves of state retail processes.
none of its current or prospective large load customers have expressed any interest in mandatory/dispatchable curtailment.
